objective: Bidirectional ping-pong frame protocol with W3C trace context propagation across iroh uni streams. Single trace_id spans gateway→broker→gateway. Producer/consumer OTel SpanKinds reflect messaging semantics.
in_scope: crates/rafka-mesh-ops: InternalMeshFrame{Ping,Pong{org_id}} with bincode 2 serde encode/decode
32-byte wire header: trace_id[16] + span_id[8] + trace_flags[1] + padding[7] + bincode frame
encode_with_context(&otel::Context) + decode_with_context() helpers for W3C traceparent propagation
Arc<DashMap<String,Connection>> peer registry in both gateway and broker
mdns dedup guard: registry.contains_key before re-dial (fixes Sprint 03 re-publish storm)
Gateway run_ping_sender: every 10s, encode Ping with context, open_uni, emit rafka.mesh.frame.sent{otel.kind=producer}
Broker run_frame_reader: accept_uni loop, decode_with_context, emit rafka.mesh.frame.received{otel.kind=consumer}, reply Pong
Gateway run_frame_reader: accept_uni loop, decode_with_context, emit rafka.mesh.frame.received{otel.kind=consumer} on Pong
Broker frame.sent (pong) is a child of frame.received — chains all 4 spans under one trace_id
out_of_scope: HTTP/2 or gRPC metadata injection — wire-level binary header only
exit_criteria: 4 spans under single trace_id in Jaeger: gateway frame.sent + broker frame.received + broker frame.sent + gateway frame.received DONE
span.kind=producer on frame.sent, span.kind=consumer on frame.received DONE
rafka.mesh.frame.decode_failed span defined and wired at both receive sites DONE
